'''Ellis Longstaff''' (born 5 July 2002) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who  played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]]. He has played for the [[England Knights]] at international level.

He has previously spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]].

==Career==
Longstaff is a product of the Elite Rugby Academy and is the first individual of the academy to go professional since its initial launch in 2012.

Longstaff has previously played for Kippax Welfare ARLFC and was part of the youth development program at the [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] club.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Warrington Wolves===
Longstaff made his Super League debut in round 11 of the [[Super League XXV results|2020 Super League season]] for Warrington against [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/warrington-vs-hull/teams/54477 |title=Starting Lineups - Warrington vs Hull |publisher=Sky Sports |date=4 September 2020}}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
During the 2022 Super League season, Longstaff joined [[Hull F.C.]] on a short-term loan deal.  In round 18 of the 2022 season, Longstaff scored two tries for Hull F.C. in their 34–28 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] at [[Magic Weekend]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/jul/10/hull-kr-hull-fc-magic-weekend-super-league-match-report/|title=Magic Weekend: Late video referee controversy hands Hull FC derby win|newspaper=The Guardian|date=10 July 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>

===Salford===
In the 2023 Super League season, Longstaff played nine matches for Salford as the club finished 7th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2023/10/02/salford-red-devils-2023-season-in-review/|title=Salford Red Devils' 2023 season in review|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk|date=2 October 2023 }}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 26 July 2023, Longstaff joined [[Featherstone Rovers]] on a permanent deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-wolves-ellis-longstaff-featherstone-rovers-move/|title=Warrington Wolves starlet makes permanent Championship move after Salford Red Devils loan spell|website=Love Rugby League|date=26 Jul 2023 |accessdate=26 Jul 2023}}</ref>

===England Knights===
At the end of the 2021 season, he went on to make his England knights debut against Jamaica on the 15th October 2021.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}
